An’ thet coloured ole ’og of a cow of a mother, soon’s she’s off ther booze, ’ll see thet she gets it!” Then he missed his silk handkerchief. “Ghost!” he said, breathing heavily, “Mag’s snavelled it! Lizer ’ll spot thet’s gone soon’s we get ’ithin cooee of ’er!” Against hope he turned and looked along the road; felt every pocket, lifted his feet, and looked under the mat. She struggled desperately, but after a few mouthfuls she ceased. The weird cry from the “Bendy Tree” pierced and conquered the deep throated wind. Then a sweet dream voice whispered “Little Woman!” Soft, strong arms carried her on. Weakness aroused the melting idea that all had been a mistake, and she had been fighting with friends.
